title: ageratina gypsophila
intro: Ageratina gypsophila, also known as Skyflower, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Ageratina gypsophila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
